title: Fix AI Mode or get Google out of classrooms, advocates say
excerpt: Education advocates urge schools to cut ties with Google over AI Search.
content_html:
Education advocates urge schools to cut ties with Google over AI Search.

 

By

Chase DiBenedetto

Chase DiBenedetto

Social Good Reporter

Chase joined Mashable's Social Good team in 2020, covering online stories about digital activism, climate justice, accessibility, and media representation. Her work also captures how these conversations manifest in politics, popular culture, and fandom. Sometimes she's very funny.

Read Full Bio

 

on July 19, 2026

Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Flipboard

Child safety groups question if Google is appropriate for classroom use. Credit: Zain bin Awais/Mashable/Getty Images

Table of Contents

Millennials, the first computer generation, recall many a classroom lecture about the unreliability of Wikipedia. We were told to be wary of unverified forum posts. We were advised to seek out primary sources on the World Wide Web, and the place to start was Google. 

Now, as Gen Alpha enters AI-powered classrooms, can Google Search no longer be trusted?

A new report by child safety watchdog Common Sense Media concluded that Google's modernized, AI-powered Search poses an "unacceptable risk" to young Googlers. In fact, the report authors say, the generative AI search engine is so risky that students should stop using it completely until Google educators can turn the AI off.

You May Also Like

Google's AI Search as classroom threat 

Child safety advocates have been urging schools to ditch classroom technology for years. As generative AI embeds itself in educational products, those cries have gotten louder. But while it's easy to see the potential harm in introducing something like ChatGPT to young students, it may not be as obvious that a simple Google search exposes young learners to AI.

Google launched AI Mode for Search last year. Unlike AI Overviews' rollout, AI Mode was available to everyone, immediately, including education accounts. Both Common Sense Media and other experts Mashable spoke to said this was alarming, primarily because Google's AI Mode is powered by Gemini models — models that are age-gated in other Google experiences, including on Google's educational technology suite. 

Google Search is a tentpole product for the tech giant and an essential tool for both educators and students, Robbie Torney, senior director of AI programs at Common Sense Media, explained. "Google is an outsized player in both the search space and the education space."

The company helmed a 1:1 device movement that saw the introduction of personal devices, like Chromebooks, in schools across the country. Google Workspace for Education now powers administrative suites and classroom resources. The company later became a major evangelist for AI implementation, introducing, and then ultimately rescaling, Gemini for Education. Google now boasts millions of Workspace for Education, Chromebook, and other Google educational product users ranging from Kindergartners to post-grads.

By making generative AI fundamental to Google's products — to the point where many users don't even realize they're using it — Google has eroded years of trust, sources told Mashable.

Googling isn't so simple anymore

For one, researchers have found Google's AI Overviews are prone to repeating misinformation, an early product sticking point that has not yet been resolved by the tech giant. Much like existing problems with chatbots, sources told Mashable, the product's design suggests an air of authority that can easily confuse adults, let alone children.

In addition, Common Sense Media found that Google's AI tools readily completed homework assignments for minor users. Experts suggest that this combination of misleading design, varying responses, and the agreeable nature of chatbots (including AI Mode) may lead students to unknowingly cheat. At the bare minimum, they say, searching on Google may be making learning harder.

"They are infusing AI in developmentally inappropriate ways through Search," explained Amanda Bickerstaff, deputy director of AI for Education. The organization works with educational institutions to build out AI literacy programs and reduce the potentially negative impact of generative AI tools on children's learning. 

Mashable Light Speed

There is no reason why a 10-year-old, 13-year-old, or even 15-year-old should see an AI Overview or have AI Mode.

Bickerstaff called AI Mode a backdoor for students to access unsafe generative AI experiences. It's also complicating educators' ability to enshrine literacy skills in the next generation, Bickerstaff explained, replicating some of the worst parts of chatbot design right in a previously reliable information environment. 

"There is no reason why a 10-year-old, 13-year-old, or even 15-year-old should see an AI Overview or have AI Mode," said Bickerstaff. "When teaching AI literacy, the first step is to be intentional. These modes are the opposite. You don't go to a search engine to get a chatbot answer, you go to search for real things."

According to Common Sense Media's report, AI Overviews and Google's new Search chatbot AI Mode could also pose mental health and safety risks to children.

Google does not offer the option to disable only AI features while keeping Search accessible. That means children can still be exposed to generative AI while at school even when guardrails are in place, simply by doing their schoolwork. Parents can turn off Search entirely or block Google.com for the child accounts they manage, Google explains. School administrators could theoretically do the same for Workspace for Education accounts, bricking the Google Search domain for all users. Bickerstaff sees this as putting educators between a rock and a hard place. "What are you going to say? That kids can't search the web?"

In a statement to Mashable following the report's release, Google said Common Sense Media's stress testing was narrow and ambiguous, and not an "effective way to measure product safety and helpfulness." Common Sense Media did not test Workspace for Education accounts, only consumer accounts. However, Google expressed that the AI-powered Search experience is the same for all users, said Torney.

Could AI Search be a legal nightmare for schools?

Because of this, sources Mashable spoke to said that it may be time to cut ties with Google.

"Google’s AI Overview and AI Mode are undermining kids’ education, supplying them with dangerous disinformation, and putting their very lives at risk," said David Monahan, campaign director for child safety nonprofit Fairplay for Kids. "If Google is not even willing to disable AI features for students, schools should stop using Google products altogether."

Common Sense Media's report could be a startling wake-up call for school districts, said Anya Meksin, deputy director of Schools Beyond Screens, a national coalition pushing for school policies that limit tech use in classrooms. Schools Beyond Screens recently spearheaded new tech use policies at Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) schools, including a screen time ban for kindergarten and first grade classes. 

Both Fairplay and Schools Beyond Screens are leading calls for years-long moratoriums on Gen AI use in schools. Meksin explained that advocates have already sounded the alarm about AI Search and other integrated Google products, not just chatbots.

"We are shocked by the way Big Tech has colonized public education with their products," Meksin said. "We have to treat this as an emergency. Education is the foundation of our democracy, and they are knowingly disabling our children from being able to fight back against these companies in the future."

School districts, including LAUSD, have struggled to address the problems posed by the reliance on classroom tech, often in violation of their own burgeoning AI policies, she explained. 

"School districts are instantly in violation of any policies put in place because of the speed of the technology and the way AI is embedded," said Meksin. For example, LAUSD's new policy bans students under 13 years of age from using generative AI in school. But through products like Chromebooks and Google Search on school computers, students can still interact with AI Mode. 

We have to treat this as an emergency.

Over the last 20 years, K-12 schools have spent billions to outfit their classrooms with educational technology. Meksin said she was told by several districts that it would be impossible to fully disable such expansive services, including Google products like Chromebooks.  

"We find this position to be completely laughable," Meksin said. "School districts are liable for exposing children to products known to cause severe and lasting harm." She believes educational institutions could enter the same legal hot water as social media and AI companies. 

Google and its parent company Alphabet recently faced its first wrongful death lawsuit after a user died by suicide following conversations with Gemini. YouTube, also owned by Alphabet, was found guilty of negligent platform design and subsequent mental health harm to minor users. Torney likened the rollout of Google's AI features to the introduction of YouTube to classrooms, which has also come under fire from child safety advocates.

Instead of opening themselves up to greater liability, Meksin believes school districts should push for change from Big Tech — starting with stronger controls for embedded generative AI features now available on school devices. "As the largest purchasers of these products, districts have power. If Google refuses to comply, districts must cease using these products and void these contracts." 

Social Good Reporter

Chase joined Mashable's Social Good team in 2020, covering online stories about digital activism, climate justice, accessibility, and media representation. Her work also captures how these conversations manifest in politics, popular culture, and fandom. Sometimes she's very funny.

sentences.zh: 此外，Common Sense Media 发现，谷歌的 AI 工具可以轻松完成未成年用户的家庭作业。

sentences.en: Experts suggest that this combination of misleading design, varying responses, and the agreeable nature of chatbots (including AI Mode) may lead students to unknowingly cheat.
sentences.zh: 专家认为，误导性设计、不同的反应以及聊天机器人（包括人工智能模式）的宜人性质的结合可能会导致学生在不知不觉中作弊。

sentences.en: At the bare minimum, they say, searching on Google may be making learning harder.
sentences.zh: 他们说，至少，在谷歌上搜索可能会让学习变得更加困难。

blocks.html_en: "They are infusing AI in developmentally inappropriate ways through Search," explained Amanda Bickerstaff, deputy director of AI for Education. The organization works with educational institutions to build out AI literacy programs and reduce the potentially negative impact of generative AI tools on children's learning.

sentences.en: "They are infusing AI in developmentally inappropriate ways through Search," explained Amanda Bickerstaff, deputy director of AI for Education.
sentences.zh: “他们通过搜索以不适合发展的方式注入人工智能，”人工智能教育副主任阿曼达·比克斯塔夫（Amanda Bickerstaff）解释道。

sentences.en: The organization works with educational institutions to build out AI literacy programs and reduce the potentially negative impact of generative AI tools on children's learning.
sentences.zh: 该组织与教育机构合作制定人工智能素养计划，并减少生成式人工智能工具对儿童学习的潜在负面影响。

blocks.html_en: Mashable Light Speed

sentences.en: Mashable Light Speed
sentences.zh: 可混搭光速

blocks.html_en: Bickerstaff called AI Mode a backdoor for students to access unsafe generative AI experiences. It's also complicating educators' ability to enshrine literacy skills in the next generation, Bickerstaff explained, replicating some of the worst parts of chatbot design right in a previously reliable information environment.

sentences.en: Bickerstaff called AI Mode a backdoor for students to access unsafe generative AI experiences.
sentences.zh: 比克斯塔夫称人工智能模式是学生获得不安全的生成式人工智能体验的后门。

sentences.en: It's also complicating educators' ability to enshrine literacy skills in the next generation, Bickerstaff explained, replicating some of the worst parts of chatbot design right in a previously reliable information environment.
sentences.zh: 比克斯塔夫解释说，这也使教育工作者将识字技能传授给下一代的能力变得复杂，因为在以前可靠的信息环境中复制了聊天机器人设计中的一些最糟糕的部分。

blocks.html_en: "There is no reason why a 10-year-old, 13-year-old, or even 15-year-old should see an AI Overview or have AI Mode," said Bickerstaff. "When teaching AI literacy, the first step is to be intentional. These modes are the opposite. You don't go to a search engine to get a chatbot answer, you go to search for real things."

sentences.en: "There is no reason why a 10-year-old, 13-year-old, or even 15-year-old should see an AI Overview or have AI Mode," said Bickerstaff.
sentences.zh: Bickerstaff 表示：“10 岁、13 岁甚至 15 岁的孩子没有理由应该看到 AI 概述或使用 AI 模式。”

sentences.en: "When teaching AI literacy, the first step is to be intentional.
sentences.zh: “在教授人工智能素养时，第一步是有意识的。

sentences.en: These modes are the opposite.
sentences.zh: 这些模式是相反的。

sentences.en: You don't go to a search engine to get a chatbot answer, you go to search for real things."
sentences.zh: 你不会去搜索引擎得到聊天机器人的答案，你会去搜索真实的东西。”

blocks.html_en: According to Common Sense Media's report, AI Overviews and Google's new Search chatbot AI Mode could also pose mental health and safety risks to children.

sentences.en: According to Common Sense Media's report, AI Overviews and Google's new Search chatbot AI Mode could also pose mental health and safety risks to children.
sentences.zh: 据 Common Sense Media 报道，AI Overviews 和谷歌新的搜索聊天机器人 AI 模式也可能给儿童带来心理健康和安全风险。

blocks.html_en: Google does not offer the option to disable only AI features while keeping Search accessible. That means children can still be exposed to generative AI while at school even when guardrails are in place, simply by doing their schoolwork. Parents can turn off Search entirely or block Google.com for the child accounts they manage, Google explains. School administrators could theoretically do the same for Workspace for Education accounts, bricking the Google Search domain for all users. Bickerstaff sees this as putting educators between a rock and a hard place. "What are you going to say? That kids can't search the web?"

sentences.en: Google does not offer the option to disable only AI features while keeping Search accessible.
sentences.zh: 谷歌并没有提供在保持搜索可访问性的同时仅禁用人工智能功能的选项。

sentences.en: That means children can still be exposed to generative AI while at school even when guardrails are in place, simply by doing their schoolwork.
sentences.zh: 这意味着即使有护栏，孩子们在学校仍然可以接触到生成式人工智能，只需做作业即可。

sentences.en: Parents can turn off Search entirely or block Google.com for the child accounts they manage, Google explains.
sentences.zh: 谷歌解释说，家长可以完全关闭搜索或阻止他们管理的儿童帐户的Google.com。

sentences.en: School administrators could theoretically do the same for Workspace for Education accounts, bricking the Google Search domain for all users.
sentences.zh: 理论上，学校管理员可以对 Workspace for Education 帐户执行相同的操作，为所有用户屏蔽 Google 搜索域。

sentences.en: Bickerstaff sees this as putting educators between a rock and a hard place.
sentences.zh: 比克斯塔夫认为这让教育工作者陷入进退两难的境地。

sentences.en: "What are you going to say?
sentences.zh: “你要说什么？

sentences.en: That kids can't search the web?"
sentences.zh: 孩子们不能搜索网络吗？”

blocks.html_en: In a statement to Mashable following the report's release, Google said Common Sense Media's stress testing was narrow and ambiguous, and not an "effective way to measure product safety and helpfulness." Common Sense Media did not test Workspace for Education accounts, only consumer accounts. However, Google expressed that the AI-powered Search experience is the same for all users, said Torney.

sentences.en: In a statement to Mashable following the report's release, Google said Common Sense Media's stress testing was narrow and ambiguous, and not an "effective way to measure product safety and helpfulness."
sentences.zh: 报告发布后，谷歌在给 Mashable 的一份声明中表示，Common Sense Media 的压力测试范围狭窄且含糊不清，并不是“衡量产品安全性和有用性的有效方法”。

sentences.en: Common Sense Media did not test Workspace for Education accounts, only consumer accounts.
sentences.zh: Common Sense Media 未测试 Workspace for Education 帐户，仅测试消费者帐户。

sentences.en: However, Google expressed that the AI-powered Search experience is the same for all users, said Torney.
sentences.zh: 然而，托尼表示，谷歌表示，人工智能驱动的搜索体验对所有用户来说都是相同的。

blocks.html_en: Could AI Search be a legal nightmare for schools?

sentences.en: Could AI Search be a legal nightmare for schools?
sentences.zh: 人工智能搜索会成为学校的法律噩梦吗？

blocks.html_en: Because of this, sources Mashable spoke to said that it may be time to cut ties with Google.

sentences.en: Because of this, sources Mashable spoke to said that it may be time to cut ties with Google.
sentences.zh: 因此，Mashable 采访的消息人士表示，可能是时候切断与谷歌的联系了。

blocks.html_en: "Google’s AI Overview and AI Mode are undermining kids’ education, supplying them with dangerous disinformation, and putting their very lives at risk," said David Monahan, campaign director for child safety nonprofit Fairplay for Kids. "If Google is not even willing to disable AI features for students, schools should stop using Google products altogether."

sentences.en: "Google’s AI Overview and AI Mode are undermining kids’ education, supplying them with dangerous disinformation, and putting their very lives at risk," said David Monahan, campaign director for child safety nonprofit Fairplay for Kids.
sentences.zh: 儿童安全非盈利组织 Fairplay for Kids 的活动总监戴维·莫纳汉 (David Monahan) 表示：“谷歌的人工智能概述和人工智能模式正在破坏孩子们的教育，向他们提供危险的虚假信息，并将他们的生命置于危险之中。”

sentences.en: "If Google is not even willing to disable AI features for students, schools should stop using Google products altogether."
sentences.zh: “如果谷歌甚至不愿意为学生禁用人工智能功能，学校就应该完全停止使用谷歌产品。”

blocks.html_en: Common Sense Media's report could be a startling wake-up call for school districts, said Anya Meksin, deputy director of Schools Beyond Screens, a national coalition pushing for school policies that limit tech use in classrooms. Schools Beyond Screens recently spearheaded new tech use policies at Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) schools, including a screen time ban for kindergarten and first grade classes.

sentences.en: Common Sense Media's report could be a startling wake-up call for school districts, said Anya Meksin, deputy director of Schools Beyond Screens, a national coalition pushing for school policies that limit tech use in classrooms.
sentences.zh: 超越屏幕学校 (Schools Beyond Screens) 的副主任安雅·梅克辛 (Anya Meksin) 表示，Common Sense Media 的报告可能会给学区敲响警钟。该联盟是一个推动限制课堂科技使用的学校政策的全国联盟。

sentences.en: Schools Beyond Screens recently spearheaded new tech use policies at Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) schools, including a screen time ban for kindergarten and first grade classes.
sentences.zh: Schools Beyond Screens 最近在洛杉矶联合学区 (LAUSD) 学校率先推行了新技术使用政策，包括幼儿园和一年级班级的屏幕时间禁令。

blocks.html_en: Both Fairplay and Schools Beyond Screens are leading calls for years-long moratoriums on Gen AI use in schools. Meksin explained that advocates have already sounded the alarm about AI Search and other integrated Google products, not just chatbots.

sentences.en: Both Fairplay and Schools Beyond Screens are leading calls for years-long moratoriums on Gen AI use in schools.
sentences.zh: Fairplay 和 Schools Beyond Screens 都带头呼吁在学校中多年暂停使用人工智能。

sentences.en: Meksin explained that advocates have already sounded the alarm about AI Search and other integrated Google products, not just chatbots.
sentences.zh: 梅克辛解释说，倡导者已经对人工智能搜索和其他集成的谷歌产品敲响了警钟，而不仅仅是聊天机器人。

blocks.html_en: "We are shocked by the way Big Tech has colonized public education with their products," Meksin said. "We have to treat this as an emergency. Education is the foundation of our democracy, and they are knowingly disabling our children from being able to fight back against these companies in the future."

sentences.en: "We are shocked by the way Big Tech has colonized public education with their products," Meksin said.
sentences.zh: 梅克辛说：“我们对大型科技公司用他们的产品殖民公共教育的方式感到震惊。”

sentences.en: "We have to treat this as an emergency.
sentences.zh: “我们必须将此视为紧急情况。

sentences.en: Education is the foundation of our democracy, and they are knowingly disabling our children from being able to fight back against these companies in the future."
sentences.zh: 教育是我们民主的基础，他们故意让我们的孩子无法在未来反击这些公司。”

blocks.html_en: School districts, including LAUSD, have struggled to address the problems posed by the reliance on classroom tech, often in violation of their own burgeoning AI policies, she explained.

sentences.en: School districts, including LAUSD, have struggled to address the problems posed by the reliance on classroom tech, often in violation of their own burgeoning AI policies, she explained.
sentences.zh: 她解释说，包括洛杉矶联合学区在内的学区一直在努力解决因依赖课堂技术而带来的问题，这往往违反了他们自己新兴的人工智能政策。

blocks.html_en: "School districts are instantly in violation of any policies put in place because of the speed of the technology and the way AI is embedded," said Meksin. For example, LAUSD's new policy bans students under 13 years of age from using generative AI in school. But through products like Chromebooks and Google Search on school computers, students can still interact with AI Mode.

sentences.en: "School districts are instantly in violation of any policies put in place because of the speed of the technology and the way AI is embedded," said Meksin.
sentences.zh: 梅克辛说：“由于技术的速度和人工智能的嵌入方式，学区立即违反了任何已制定的政策。”

sentences.en: For example, LAUSD's new policy bans students under 13 years of age from using generative AI in school.
sentences.zh: 例如，洛杉矶联合学区的新政策禁止 13 岁以下的学生在学校使用生成式人工智能。

sentences.en: But through products like Chromebooks and Google Search on school computers, students can still interact with AI Mode.
sentences.zh: 但通过学校计算机上的 Chromebook 和 Google 搜索等产品，学生仍然可以与 AI 模式进行交互。

blocks.html_en: Over the last 20 years, K-12 schools have spent billions to outfit their classrooms with educational technology. Meksin said she was told by several districts that it would be impossible to fully disable such expansive services, including Google products like Chromebooks.

sentences.en: Over the last 20 years, K-12 schools have spent billions to outfit their classrooms with educational technology.
sentences.zh: 过去 20 年来，K-12 学校花费了数十亿美元为教室配备教育技术。

sentences.en: Meksin said she was told by several districts that it would be impossible to fully disable such expansive services, including Google products like Chromebooks.
sentences.zh: 梅克辛说，几个地区告诉她，完全禁用如此广泛的服务是不可能的，包括 Chromebook 等谷歌产品。

blocks.html_en: "We find this position to be completely laughable," Meksin said. "School districts are liable for exposing children to products known to cause severe and lasting harm." She believes educational institutions could enter the same legal hot water as social media and AI companies.

sentences.en: "We find this position to be completely laughable," Meksin said.
sentences.zh: “我们发现这个立场完全可笑，”梅克辛说。

sentences.en: "School districts are liable for exposing children to products known to cause severe and lasting harm."
sentences.zh: “学区有责任让儿童接触已知会造成严重和持久伤害的产品。”

sentences.en: She believes educational institutions could enter the same legal hot water as social media and AI companies.
sentences.zh: 她认为，教育机构可能会像社交媒体和人工智能公司一样陷入法律困境。

blocks.html_en: Google and its parent company Alphabet recently faced its first wrongful death lawsuit after a user died by suicide following conversations with Gemini. YouTube, also owned by Alphabet, was found guilty of negligent platform design and subsequent mental health harm to minor users. Torney likened the rollout of Google's AI features to the introduction of YouTube to classrooms, which has also come under fire from child safety advocates.

sentences.en: Google and its parent company Alphabet recently faced its first wrongful death lawsuit after a user died by suicide following conversations with Gemini.
sentences.zh: 谷歌及其母公司 Alphabet 最近面临首例非正常死亡诉讼，一名用户在与 Gemini 交谈后自杀身亡。

sentences.en: YouTube, also owned by Alphabet, was found guilty of negligent platform design and subsequent mental health harm to minor users.
sentences.zh: 同样属于 Alphabet 旗下的 YouTube 被判犯有平台设计疏忽以及随后对未成年用户造成心理健康伤害的罪名。

sentences.en: Torney likened the rollout of Google's AI features to the introduction of YouTube to classrooms, which has also come under fire from child safety advocates.
sentences.zh: 托尼将谷歌人工智能功能的推出比作将 YouTube 引入课堂，这也遭到了儿童安全倡导者的批评。

blocks.html_en: Instead of opening themselves up to greater liability, Meksin believes school districts should push for change from Big Tech — starting with stronger controls for embedded generative AI features now available on school devices. "As the largest purchasers of these products, districts have power. If Google refuses to comply, districts must cease using these products and void these contracts."

sentences.en: Instead of opening themselves up to greater liability, Meksin believes school districts should push for change from Big Tech — starting with stronger controls for embedded generative AI features now available on school devices.
sentences.zh: 梅克辛认为，学区不应承担更大的责任，而应推动大型科技公司的变革——首先对学校设备上现有的嵌入式生成人工智能功能进行更强有力的控制。

sentences.en: "As the largest purchasers of these products, districts have power.
sentences.zh: “作为这些产品的最大购买者，各地区拥有权力。

sentences.en: If Google refuses to comply, districts must cease using these products and void these contracts."
sentences.zh: 如果谷歌拒绝遵守，学区必须停止使用这些产品并废除这些合同。”

blocks.html_en: Social Good Reporter

sentences.en: Social Good Reporter
sentences.zh: 社会公益记者

blocks.html_en: Chase joined Mashable's Social Good team in 2020, covering online stories about digital activism, climate justice, accessibility, and media representation. Her work also captures how these conversations manifest in politics, popular culture, and fandom. Sometimes she's very funny.

sentences.en: Chase joined Mashable's Social Good team in 2020, covering online stories about digital activism, climate justice, accessibility, and media representation.
sentences.zh: Chase 于 2020 年加入 Mashable 的社会公益团队，报道有关数字行动主义、气候正义、可访问性和媒体代表性的在线故事。

sentences.en: Her work also captures how these conversations manifest in politics, popular culture, and fandom.
sentences.zh: 她的作品还捕捉了这些对话如何在政治、流行文化和粉丝圈中体现。

sentences.en: Sometimes she's very funny.
sentences.zh: 有时她很有趣。

blocks.html_en: These newsletters may contain advertising, deals, or affiliate links. By clicking Subscribe, you confirm you are 16+ and agree to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.

sentences.en: These newsletters may contain advertising, deals, or affiliate links.
sentences.zh: 这些新闻通讯可能包含广告、优惠或附属链接。

sentences.en: By clicking Subscribe, you confirm you are 16+ and agree to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.
sentences.zh: 单击“订阅”即表示您确认您已年满 16 岁并同意我们的使用条款和隐私政策。

content_html_zh:
教育倡导者敦促学校在人工智能搜索问题上切断与谷歌的联系。

 

By

蔡斯·迪贝内代托

蔡斯·迪贝内代托

社会公益记者

Chase 于 2020 年加入 Mashable 的社会公益团队，报道有关数字行动主义、气候正义、可访问性和媒体代表性的在线故事。 她的作品还捕捉了这些对话如何在政治、流行文化和粉丝圈中体现。 有时她很有趣。

阅读完整的简历

 

2026 年 7 月 19 日

分享到 Facebook 分享到 Twitter 分享到 Flipboard

儿童安全组织质疑 Google 是否适合课堂使用。 图片来源：Zain bin Awais/Mashable/Getty Images

目录

千禧一代，第一代计算机一代，记得许多关于维基百科不可靠性的课堂讲座。 我们被告知要警惕未经验证的论坛帖子。 我们被建议在万维网上寻找主要资源，而从谷歌开始。

现在，随着 Gen Alpha 进入人工智能课堂，谷歌搜索是否不再值得信任？

儿童安全监管机构 Common Sense Media 的一份新报告得出的结论是，谷歌现代化的人工智能搜索对年轻的谷歌员工构成了“不可接受的风险”。 事实上，报告作者表示，生成式人工智能搜索引擎风险太大，学生应该完全停止使用它，直到谷歌教育工作者可以关闭人工智能。

你可能也喜欢

谷歌的人工智能搜索对课堂构成威胁

儿童安全倡导者多年来一直敦促学校放弃课堂技术。 随着生成式人工智能嵌入到教育产品中，这些呼声越来越大。 但是，虽然很容易看出向年轻学生介绍 ChatGPT 之类的潜在危害，但简单的 Google 搜索让年轻学习者接触人工智能可能并不那么明显。

谷歌去年推出了人工智能搜索模式。 与人工智能概述的推出不同，人工智能模式立即可供所有人使用，包括教育帐户。 Common Sense Media 和 Mashable 采访的其他专家都表示，这令人担忧，主要是因为 Google 的 AI 模式由 Gemini 模型提供支持，这些模型在 Google 的其他体验（包括 Google 的教育技术套件）中受到年龄限制。

Common Sense Media 人工智能项目高级总监 Robbie Torney 解释说，谷歌搜索是这家科技巨头的主力产品，也是教育工作者和学生的重要工具。 “谷歌在搜索领域和教育领域都是一个巨大的参与者。”

该公司发起了一场 1:1 设备运动，在全国各地的学校中引入了 Chromebook 等个人设备。 Google Workspace 教育版现在为管理套件和课堂资源提供支持。 该公司后来成为人工智能实施的主要传播者，引入并最终重新扩展了 Gemini for Education。 Google 现在拥有数百万教育工作区、Chromebook 和其他 Google 教育产品用户，涵盖从幼儿园到研究生的范围。

消息人士告诉 Mashable，通过将生成式人工智能作为谷歌产品的基础（以至于许多用户甚至没有意识到他们正在使用它），谷歌已经侵蚀了多年来的信任。

谷歌搜索不再那么简单

首先，研究人员发现谷歌的人工智能概述很容易重复错误信息，这是这家科技巨头尚未解决的早期产品症结所在。 消息人士告诉 Mashable，与聊天机器人现有的问题非常相似，该产品的设计呈现出一种权威的气息，很容易让成年人感到困惑，更不用说儿童了。

此外，Common Sense Media 发现，谷歌的 AI 工具可以轻松完成未成年用户的家庭作业。 专家认为，误导性设计、不同的反应以及聊天机器人（包括人工智能模式）的宜人性质的结合可能会导致学生在不知不觉中作弊。 他们说，至少，在谷歌上搜索可能会让学习变得更加困难。

“他们通过搜索以不适合发展的方式注入人工智能，”人工智能教育副主任阿曼达·比克斯塔夫（Amanda Bickerstaff）解释道。 该组织与教育机构合作制定人工智能素养计划，并减少生成式人工智能工具对儿童学习的潜在负面影响。

可混搭光速

There is no reason why a 10-year-old, 13-year-old, or even 15-year-old should see an AI Overview or have AI Mode.

比克斯塔夫称人工智能模式是学生获得不安全的生成式人工智能体验的后门。 比克斯塔夫解释说，这也使教育工作者将识字技能传授给下一代的能力变得复杂，因为在以前可靠的信息环境中复制了聊天机器人设计中的一些最糟糕的部分。

Bickerstaff 表示：“10 岁、13 岁甚至 15 岁的孩子没有理由应该看到 AI 概述或使用 AI 模式。” “在教授人工智能素养时，第一步是有意识的。 这些模式是相反的。 你不会去搜索引擎得到聊天机器人的答案，你会去搜索真实的东西。”

据 Common Sense Media 报道，AI Overviews 和谷歌新的搜索聊天机器人 AI 模式也可能给儿童带来心理健康和安全风险。

谷歌并没有提供在保持搜索可访问性的同时仅禁用人工智能功能的选项。 这意味着即使有护栏，孩子们在学校仍然可以接触到生成式人工智能，只需做作业即可。 谷歌解释说，家长可以完全关闭搜索或阻止他们管理的儿童帐户的Google.com。 理论上，学校管理员可以对 Workspace for Education 帐户执行相同的操作，为所有用户屏蔽 Google 搜索域。 比克斯塔夫认为这让教育工作者陷入进退两难的境地。 “你要说什么？ 孩子们不能搜索网络吗？”

报告发布后，谷歌在给 Mashable 的一份声明中表示，Common Sense Media 的压力测试范围狭窄且含糊不清，并不是“衡量产品安全性和有用性的有效方法”。 Common Sense Media 未测试 Workspace for Education 帐户，仅测试消费者帐户。 然而，托尼表示，谷歌表示，人工智能驱动的搜索体验对所有用户来说都是相同的。

人工智能搜索会成为学校的法律噩梦吗？

因此，Mashable 采访的消息人士表示，可能是时候切断与谷歌的联系了。

儿童安全非盈利组织 Fairplay for Kids 的活动总监戴维·莫纳汉 (David Monahan) 表示：“谷歌的人工智能概述和人工智能模式正在破坏孩子们的教育，向他们提供危险的虚假信息，并将他们的生命置于危险之中。” “如果谷歌甚至不愿意为学生禁用人工智能功能，学校就应该完全停止使用谷歌产品。”

超越屏幕学校 (Schools Beyond Screens) 的副主任安雅·梅克辛 (Anya Meksin) 表示，Common Sense Media 的报告可能会给学区敲响警钟。该联盟是一个推动限制课堂科技使用的学校政策的全国联盟。 Schools Beyond Screens 最近在洛杉矶联合学区 (LAUSD) 学校率先推行了新技术使用政策，包括幼儿园和一年级班级的屏幕时间禁令。

Fairplay 和 Schools Beyond Screens 都带头呼吁在学校中多年暂停使用人工智能。 梅克辛解释说，倡导者已经对人工智能搜索和其他集成的谷歌产品敲响了警钟，而不仅仅是聊天机器人。

梅克辛说：“我们对大型科技公司用他们的产品殖民公共教育的方式感到震惊。” “我们必须将此视为紧急情况。 教育是我们民主的基础，他们故意让我们的孩子无法在未来反击这些公司。”

她解释说，包括洛杉矶联合学区在内的学区一直在努力解决因依赖课堂技术而带来的问题，这往往违反了他们自己新兴的人工智能政策。

梅克辛说：“由于技术的速度和人工智能的嵌入方式，学区立即违反了任何已制定的政策。” 例如，洛杉矶联合学区的新政策禁止 13 岁以下的学生在学校使用生成式人工智能。 但通过学校计算机上的 Chromebook 和 Google 搜索等产品，学生仍然可以与 AI 模式进行交互。

We have to treat this as an emergency.

过去 20 年来，K-12 学校花费了数十亿美元为教室配备教育技术。 梅克辛说，几个地区告诉她，完全禁用如此广泛的服务是不可能的，包括 Chromebook 等谷歌产品。

“我们发现这个立场完全可笑，”梅克辛说。 “学区有责任让儿童接触已知会造成严重和持久伤害的产品。” 她认为，教育机构可能会像社交媒体和人工智能公司一样陷入法律困境。

谷歌及其母公司 Alphabet 最近面临首例非正常死亡诉讼，一名用户在与 Gemini 交谈后自杀身亡。 同样属于 Alphabet 旗下的 YouTube 被判犯有平台设计疏忽以及随后对未成年用户造成心理健康伤害的罪名。 托尼将谷歌人工智能功能的推出比作将 YouTube 引入课堂，这也遭到了儿童安全倡导者的批评。

梅克辛认为，学区不应承担更大的责任，而应推动大型科技公司的变革——首先对学校设备上现有的嵌入式生成人工智能功能进行更强有力的控制。 “作为这些产品的最大购买者，各地区拥有权力。 如果谷歌拒绝遵守，学区必须停止使用这些产品并废除这些合同。”

社会公益记者

Chase 于 2020 年加入 Mashable 的社会公益团队，报道有关数字行动主义、气候正义、可访问性和媒体代表性的在线故事。 她的作品还捕捉了这些对话如何在政治、流行文化和粉丝圈中体现。 有时她很有趣。
